text: Statistical Learning Approach to Optimal Robust Countermeasures of Cyber-Physical System Against Multiple Attacks For different attack scenarios, each with a unique attack vector representing malicious activities behind attacks on power grid, measurements obtained from the batch and online observations are formulated into three classic attack detection problems by the means of statistical learning theory. Based on the mathematical representation of measurement models associated with normal and malicious operating conditions, each of the attack detection problems is transformed into a machine learning classification problem by modeling secure and attack measurements as distinguishable classes. Accordingly, a detection framework has been put forward based on possible prior knowledge of power system and the sparse nature of attack measurements. As part of the solution, classical batch and online learning techniques including supervised and semisupervised learning have been formulated at the decision and feature level fusions in order to tackle the attack detection problem. Additionally, in this research, statistical and geometric properties associated with the attack vectors related to various attack scenarios are matched against the employed learning techniques to exploit the efficiency of statistical learning theory for detecting unobservable attacks in smart grids. Numerical studies carried out on modified IEEE test systems show the superior performance of the learning techniques utilized in this work and, hence, demonstrate that the machine learning algorithms yield more accurate results compared with well-known vector state estimation attack detection methods provided in the literature for the proposed detection framework.
explanation: The passage uses broad, generic statements with few concrete technical details and some awkward phrasing, a pattern often seen in AI-generated text. It lacks specific methodology/results that would typically appear in a human-authored research abstract.
